rationale: Major friction across the board: the archetype's muted earth-tone palette (navy/brown/olive) and architectural cross-section visual style clash directly with Collect Em All's bright, candy-saturated 2D ball-clearing aesthetic. The "hidden bunker reveal" hook is a narrative/base-building curiosity device with no natural bridge to a line-drawing color-match loop — forcing it would require a contrived "what's hidden under the board" twist that misrepresents the gameplay and risks IPM penalties. Only soft overlap is broad casual-female curiosity appeal; recommend skipping this archetype and instead testing satisfying-ASMR or oddly-satisfying-reveal hooks that match the game's calm/saturated DNA.
